novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship constructive object is a TU/strongly-polynomial lifted flow certificate, but Exhibit 9.2 displays a compressed matrix with -2 coefficients and only describes the actual shell-lifted TU matrix in prose; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until the certificate is computed from primitives.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation-axis delta is only partial: delta_summary promises explicit lifted LP/TU and dual computation, but the exhibited TU certificate and strict-gap arithmetic are not internally consistent enough to deliver the upgrade.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.2 calls the displayed matrix a TU witness, but the shown coefficient matrix contains -2 entries, so it is not total unimodular; the shell-lifted TU matrix must be displayed and checked instead.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.3's separate outer-window upper value 0.875 is not sharp under the stated loose budgets: setting the zero-outcome B atom to 0 gives outer value 1, so the reported support-function gap arithmetic is wrong.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Exhibit 9.2 with the actual shell-expanded node-arc incidence matrix, including all split score arcs, and verify every column has the claimed network/TU form after rational scaling.

Recompute Exhibit 9.3 as a true support-function optimization: either add lower-retention/cap constraints that make the reported separate solutions sharp, or update the separate and joint support values.
State a concrete complexity theorem for Conjecture 2 with input size, lifted-network size, and the exact strongly-polynomial algorithm class being invoked.

recommended_next_step: Revise the computational exhibits so the TU certificate and strict-gap witness are computed correctly from the Section 6 primitives.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal has the right computational upgrade shape, but the load-bearing TU and dual-gap exhibits do not yet deliver the promised explicit certificate.
